Trademark registration in India is essential to protect your business name, logo, or symbol. It grants you the exclusive legal right to prevent others from using or imitating your brand identity. The process begins with a detailed trademark search to ensure the availability of your name or logo. At BookMyTM, we specialize in assisting you through every step of the trademark registration process across Kerala, Haryana, New Delhi, Rajasthan, and Uttar Pradesh.

Our experienced team helps you file your trademark application with IP India, a division of the Government of India. We handle all the required documentation and guide you in selecting the appropriate trademark class from the 45 available categories. Once the application is filed, we meticulously track the trademark status until you receive your trademark certificate. Your registered trademark will enjoy protection for ten years, with an easy renewal option to maintain its validity.

If you require global brand protection, BookMyTM facilitates international trademark registration under the Madrid Protocol, simplifying the process of securing your trademark in multiple countries. We also manage the Power of Attorney required for trademark filing and provide additional services such as tax filing for companies or LLPs. By registering your trademark, you establish ownership of an intangible asset and deter unauthorized use of your brand.
Safeguarding your intellectual property offers long-term advantages for your business growth and security.

items.a: A trademark is a unique sign, design, or expression which identifies products or services of a particular source from those of others. It can be a word, logo, slogan, sound, or even a colour combination. For example, the brand names 'TATA' or the 'Swoosh' logo of Nike.

items.q: Why is trademark registration important for my business?
items.a: Trademark registration gives you the exclusive legal right to use your brand name or logo for the goods and services you offer. It prevents competitors from using a confusingly similar mark, helps build brand value and goodwill, and serves as a valuable intangible asset.

items.q: What is the difference between the symbols TM, SM, and R®?
items.a: TM is for unregistered trademarks, SM for service marks, and R® is only for officially registered trademarks. Using R® without registration is a criminal offense.

items.q: What can be trademarked in India?
items.a: Any mark that is capable of being represented graphically and distinguishing the goods or services of one person from another can be trademarked. This includes device marks (logos), word marks, slogans, sound marks (like the ICICI jingle), three-dimensional marks, and combinations of colours.

The Trademark Registry classifies all goods and services into 45 different classes according to the NICE classification. You must file your trademark application under the correct class(es) relevant to your business. For example, Class 25 is for clothing, while Class 42 is for computer software services.

items.q: What is the process of trademark registration in India?
items.a: Search -> Filing (TM-A) -> Vienna Codification -> Examination -> Publication -> Opposition -> Registration.

items.q: How long does it take to get a trademark registered?
items.a: If the process is smooth with no objections or oppositions, it can take anywhere from 8 to 18 months. However, you can start using the TM symbol as soon as your application is filed.

An objection is raised by the Trademark Examiner during the examination stage. It could be on 'absolute grounds' (e.g., the mark is descriptive) or 'relative grounds' (e.g., it's similar to an existing mark). You must file a formal reply to the examination report within 30 days.
After the mark is published in the Trademark Journal, any third party can file an opposition within four months if they believe the registration would harm their own brand or is against the law.

items.q: For how long is a trademark registration valid?
items.a: A trademark registration is valid for 10 years from the date of application.

items.q: How do I renew my trademark?
items.a: You can renew the trademark for another 10-year period by filing a renewal form (TM-R) and paying the required fees. This can be done within one year before the expiry of the registration.

items.q: Can a trademark be registered on a personal name?
items.a: Yes, a trademark can be registered in the name of an individual, a sole proprietorship, a partnership firm, an LLP, or a company.

Trademark infringement is the unauthorized use of a registered trademark, or a confusingly similar mark, in relation to goods or services for which the mark is registered, which is likely to cause confusion among consumers.

items.q: Do I need to register my trademark in other countries separately?
items.a: Yes. Trademark rights are territorial. A trademark registered in India is only valid in India. To protect your brand in other countries, you need to apply for registration in each of those countries or use an international system like the Madrid Protocol.

ISO (International Organization for Standardization) certification is a seal of approval from a third-party body that a company runs to one of the internationally recognized ISO management systems. It proves that your business has robust, quality-assured processes in place.

items.q: Why should my business get ISO certified?
items.a: Benefits include improved efficiency, better product and service quality, increased customer trust, access to new markets (especially for tenders and international trade), and a stronger brand reputation.
